signals.title: Verenigd Koninkrijk verlaagt kapitaalbuffers voor stablecoins onder MiCA-niveau
signals.summary: UK to lower stablecoin capital buffers, undercutting EU's MiCA requirements

signals.title: Taiwan stelt eerste regelgeving voor cryptovaluta en stablecoins vast
signals.summary: Taiwan’s legislative branch has passed the nation’s first crypto and stablecoin rules, which aim to integrate the country with the global crypto market.

signals.title: EBA publiceert ontwerpmethodologie voor boeterollen onder MiCA
signals.summary: ​The European Banking Authority (EBA) published today a Consultation Paper with a draft methodology for setting fines in its role as supervisor under MiCA. The objective is to ensure that fines imposed on issuers of significant crypto-assets are consistent, proportionate and transparent, and effectively support compliance with the regulatory framework.

signals.title: Europa herziet MiCA na verstrijken juli-deadline
signals.summary: Europe is rewriting its landmark crypto rulebook MiCA as hard July 1 deadline passes

signals.title: Laatste MiCA-goedkeuringen markeren einde EU-transitieperiode
signals.summary: A final wave of MiCA approvals expanded Europe's roster of licensed crypto firms as the transitional period came to a close.

signals.title: Crédit Agricole brengt EURXT euro-stablecoin op Ethereum uit
signals.summary: Crédit Agricole’s CACEIS launches a euro-pegged stablecoin on Ethereum with 20.02 million tokens issued, targeting institutional flows and tokenized fund access.

signals.title: MiCA-regelgeving volledig van kracht: gevolgen voor gebruikers en bedrijven
signals.summary: With the July 1 deadline now passed, how will the end of MiCA's final transition phase affect users, companies, and the market?

signals.title: EBA start consultatie over prestatie-indicatoren Taxonomy-publicaties
signals.summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today published a Discussion Paper on certain key performance indicators (KPIs) and other aspects of the Disclosures Delegated Act, under Article 8 of the Taxonomy Regulation. The aim is to consult the public on potential measures to simplify and enhance the usability of information disclosed by credit institutions and investment firms. The consultation is open until 12 August 2026.
